hard.next_step: Jun 25, 2026: Received requested metrics from Sandra for QIT. Forward initial quote to Sandra to be factored as additional evaluation criteria. RFP to be issued to shortlisted vendors, exact date not provided.
hard.ais_why: Sept 10 2025 spend-analytics demo was well received on spend miner, but buyer questions were about dashboards, taxonomy and drill-downs, not AI outcomes. Scope is narrow (Spend + CLM at $110K, Merlin only). The 27 Feb 2026 CLM follow-up shows AI shown but not demanded; buyer asks How can I drill? not how does AI act?

north_star_verdict.headline: Live in Norwegian's CLM RFP with the cost estimate delivered, but the Round 2 RFP invite is unscheduled, strategic sponsor Prince Bedell has gone quiet since 15 May, and no economic buyer or competitor is named.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 27 Feb 2026 (129 days old); qualified 26 Mar; in Formal Evaluation since 24 Apr (~73 days in stage). Close date 19 Nov 2026 is 136 days out. RFI submitted 10 Jun, initial CLM cost estimate delivered 29 Jun. Required pace to hold 19 Nov: shortlist/RFP invite confirmed within ~14 days, Round 2 RFP and demos through Aug-Sep, vendor selection Sep-Oct, commercials/legal Oct-Nov. Achievable if the RFP issues soon, but the buyer's mid-May RFP target already slipped with no new date and the strategic sponsor cooled, so momentum, not the date, is the problem.
evidence: LastActivityDate 29 Jun 2026 (rep-side): Marc emailed Sandra the initial annual cost estimate; buyer reply pending
Next Step 23-25 Jun: Sandra Hill requested a total cost estimate to be factored as additional evaluation criteria; RFP to shortlisted vendors, exact date not provided
Next Step 10 Jun: Round 1 RFI submitted; shortlisted vendors to be invited to the upcoming RFP
Emails 5-18 Jun: Marc chased pricing metrics 17-18 Jun and Sandra did not respond; Horizon invite to Prince 5 Jun drew no reply
Prince Bedell last engaged 15 May (RE: Gartner Event & CLM RFP: 'legal is involved so patience is required on the timeline'); no direct Prince engagement since
Contact roles: Sandra Hill (Senior Category Manager - IT & Digital Experience), Prince Bedell (Head of Global Procurement Center of Excellence); no Economic Buyer or C-level contact role
Two buyer calls read: 27 Feb 2026 CLM intro (Prince) and 10 Sep 2025 spend demo (Prince, Adriana Cano, Michelle DiGioia and team); competitive field never named on either

metrics.narrative: The operational pain is clear but never quantified. On the 10 Sep 2025 demo Adriana Cano (Senior Director Supply Chain Technology) confirmed 'we currently do not have a centralized dashboard, and everything is just Excels,' and the team pulls spend reporting from multiple disconnected source systems and Business Objects. No contract-leakage rate, manual-hours figure, cycle-time target, or savings goal has been stated for the CLM project. Sandra Hill's 23 Jun request for a total cost estimate shows the buyer is building a cost model, but with no co-developed value case the risk is that price becomes the decision. Quantifying CLM cycle-time, contract-leakage and compliance savings against the Excel-based baseline is the move.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is mapped. On the 27 Feb 2026 intro call Prince Bedell described a department-wide transformation led by a newly hired chief supply chain officer ('She was hired to bring in transformation... She brought me in to join her'), which points to the CSO as the likely budget sponsor, but that person is never named in Salesforce, on any call, or in any activity, so no name can be asserted. Prince's 15 May note that 'legal is involved' suggests approvals will run through procurement and legal leadership, but the actual CLM budget owner is unconfirmed. Engagement sits at procurement-manager level (Sandra Hill) with no line to power. Getting Prince to name and introduce the CLM budget owner is the move.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are emerging but only partly visible. Cost is an explicit criterion: Sandra Hill asked on 23 Jun for a total cost estimate 'to be factored as additional evaluation criteria.' From the 10 Sep 2025 demo, ease of self-service dashboards and drill-down from category level one to SKU (level four) mattered to the team, with Michelle DiGioia pushing for a simple 'step one' prebuilt view rather than heavy customization. Taxonomy design quality was a stated priority for Prince ('this is a big one for us'). The full weighted CLM RFP criteria have not been shared; securing the RFP scoring rubric is the move.
sources: Avoma spend demo 10 Sep 2025 (Prince Bedell, Michelle DiGioia)

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal, staged procurement: intro/capability calls, an executed NDA (fully executed 4 Jun 2026), a Round 1 RFI (submitted 10 Jun), a cost-estimate request (23 Jun), and a Round 2 RFP to shortlisted vendors on a date not yet set. Ownership moved from strategic sponsor Prince Bedell (Head of Global Procurement CoE) to operational lead Sandra Hill (Senior Category Manager - IT & Digital Experience) from June, and the 15 May note confirms legal is in the loop. The chain from evaluation to award and who signs off is not documented, and the RFP timeline already slipped from the mid-May target. Mapping the approval chain and confirming the RFP issue date is the move.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are early but underway on the buyer's paper: NCLH issued its own NDA, which Marc signed and NCLH countersigned and returned executed on 4 Jun 2026, and Prince confirmed on 15 May that 'legal is involved' in the CLM RFP. This is a US new-business deal, so a missing PO is normal and not a gap. No MSA, Order Form, or SOW has been drafted because the deal has not reached vendor selection. As a single-suite CLM (iContract) plus Merlin deal the eventual paper is Order Form plus SOW, lighter than a full-suite MSA. Visibility improves once we are shortlisted and reach commercials.
sources: SF Email 27 May and 4 Jun 2026 (NDA executed)

identify_pain.narrative: The core pain is confirmed and owned by the supply-chain technology team: no centralized spend dashboard and reporting scattered across Excel and multiple source systems, stated plainly by Adriana Cano on 10 Sep 2025 ('we currently do not have a centralized dashboard, and everything is just Excels') and echoed by Michelle DiGioia wanting a single 'step one' category-to-supplier view. On the CLM side the buyer is actively sourcing a contract lifecycle solution, which Prince flagged on 27 Feb ('our team is looking for a CLM right now'). The pain is operational visibility and manual contract management, not an autonomous-AI driver. The pain owner is the transformation team under the chief supply chain officer.
sources: Avoma spend demo 10 Sep 2025 (Adriana Cano, Michelle DiGioia)

champion.narrative: Prince Bedell (Head of Global Procurement Center of Excellence) was the entry champion: he personally pulled Zycus into the CLM RFP on 27 Feb ('I will make the introduction for you guys on the CLM one because I know the team is out looking for one'), confirmed inclusion in the vendor list on 24 Apr, and replied on 15 May that the RFP was pending. But his advocacy has cooled: no direct engagement since 15 May, no reply to the 5 Jun Horizon invite, and he has not introduced the economic buyer or shared the competitive field. Operational ownership shifted to Sandra Hill, who runs the process professionally but shows no advocacy or coaching. The gap is a committed champion with power access; re-securing Prince and converting Sandra to a coach is the priority.
sources: SF Next Step 24 Apr and 15 May 2026
SF Email 5 Jun 2026 (Horizon invite, no reply)

competition.narrative: The competitive field is a genuine knowledge gap: the Salesforce competitor fields are empty and neither the 27 Feb nor the 10 Sep 2025 call named a rival, yet the 23 Jun Next Step ('RFP to be issued to shortlisted vendors') and the cost-estimate-as-criteria request confirm this is a multi-vendor, price-sensitive bake-off. No competitor may be asserted without evidence. The most concrete alternative on record is the status quo: the buyer runs contracts and spend reporting manually in Excel today, so a no-decision or phased-only outcome is a real threat. Our wedge is the organically built, AI-native spend-analytics engine plus Merlin, but we are positioning blind until the shortlist and RFP feedback are known.
sources: Avoma spend demo 10 Sep 2025 (Adriana Cano, Excel-based baseline)

deal_movement.summary: Steady procurement progression that has lost pace at the top: RFI in (10 Jun), NDA executed (4 Jun), cost estimate delivered (29 Jun), but the RFP invite is unscheduled and the strategic sponsor went quiet after 15 May.

competitive_position.summary: No competing vendor is named in any source, so the ranked field cannot be reconstructed; the confirmed picture is a multi-vendor, price-sensitive CLM RFP (Sandra's 23 Jun cost-estimate-as-criteria request) with the shortlist and Round 1 feedback undisclosed. The single most concrete current threat is inertia: Norwegian manages contracts and spend in Excel today (Adriana Cano, 10 Sep 2025), so a no-decision or scope-cut is more evidenced than any rival. Priority is to get the field and our RFI standing named before Round 2.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on the confirmed dashboard/spend-visibility pain and on ease-of-use, but the buyer wants simplicity over customization and the CLM-specific fit is unproven since the 2025 demo covered spend, not iContract.

best_practice_check.summary: We are live in a formal CLM RFP but positioning blind on a cooling, procurement-only relationship; the winning levers are getting the competitive field named, converting a price-only eval into a value case, and re-securing a real champion with power access.
flags: Get the competitive field and Round 1 RFI standing named before Round 2: ask Prince or Sandra who else is shortlisted and where we scored, so we are not positioning blind.
Convert a price-led eval into value: co-build a quantified CLM ROI (cycle time, leakage, compliance) against the Excel baseline with the CLM eval team before pricing hardens.
Re-secure a champion with power access: Prince has cooled and Sandra shows no advocacy, so reconfirm Prince's sponsorship and map a path to the transformation sponsor.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is present in scope (Merlin) and was demoed, but the buyer engages it as spend-analytics automation and dashboards, not agentic outcomes; the AI story is not the wedge that is landing.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 5.0 Moderate is accurate: the 10 Sep 2025 demo drew positive feedback on the AI spend-miner and auto-classification, but every buyer question was about dashboards, taxonomy and drill-downs, and the 27 Feb 2026 CLM follow-up shows AI shown but not demanded ('How can I drill?' not 'how does AI act?'). This is an operational-visibility buyer, AI-curious not AI-hungry.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: We're a mid-pack vendor invited into Norwegian's CLM RFP after Prince Bedell pulled us in, but the deal is drifting: the strategic sponsor has gone quiet since 15 May, the evaluation is a price-led procurement exercise run by category manager Sandra Hill, and no economic buyer or competitor is named.

win_position.text: We are a named participant in Norwegian's CLM RFP after Prince Bedell pulled us in on the 27 Feb intro call, and we kept our seat by delivering the requested cost estimate on 29 Jun.
Our only strategic sponsor, Prince Bedell, has not engaged since his 15 May email and ignored the 5 Jun Horizon invite, leaving an arms-length, procurement-only relationship.
We are positioning blind and on price: Sandra Hill asked on 23 Jun for a cost estimate to be factored as evaluation criteria, and no economic buyer or competing vendor is named.

deal_momentum.text: Round 1 RFI was submitted 10 Jun and the CLM cost estimate delivered 29 Jun, so the procurement paperwork is moving forward.
The Round 2 RFP has no issued date after the buyer's mid-May target slipped, and Sandra did not respond to the 17-18 Jun metrics request, so the next milestone is unscheduled.
No senior buyer has joined since the 10 Sep 2025 spend demo; recent contact is category-manager level and rep-initiated.

customer_commitment.text: Norwegian signed an NDA (executed 4 Jun) and is running a formal multi-vendor RFP, confirming a real funded project rather than curiosity.
Intent is procurement-driven and price-focused: no Zycus-specific preference was voiced and the CLM module has not yet been demoed to the eval team.

deal_risk.text: No economic buyer is mapped: the chief supply chain officer sponsoring the transformation was referenced by Prince on 27 Feb but never named or engaged.
The competitive field is blind and price is an explicit criterion, so we could lose Round 2 to an unseen rival's price or fit.
Scope narrowed from the 2025 broad spend/S2P exploration to a $110K CLM-only RFP, a defensive cost-cutting signal that caps the deal.
